skill.name: Analytics Tracking & Measurement Strategy
skill.description: Design, audit, and improve analytics tracking systems that produce reliable decision-ready data.
skill.summary: Expert guidance for GA4, GTM, and product analytics implementation with focus on measurement strategy and data quality.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This is a prompt-only skill containing only documentation and guidance in SKILL.md. No executable code, scripts, network calls, or filesystem operations detected. Static analysis correctly identified zero security risks. The skill provides analytics measurement strategy guidance without any code execution capabilities.

content.value_statement: Many teams track data that cannot be trusted for decisions. This skill designs measurement strategies that produce accurate, decision-ready analytics data.
actual_capabilities: Calculate Measurement Readiness and Signal Quality Index scores
Design event taxonomies with clear naming conventions
Define conversion strategies with proper counting rules
Audit existing analytics setups for data quality issues
Create tracking plans mapped to business decisions
Provide GA4 and GTM implementation guidance
limitations: Does not implement tracking code directly in your codebase
Does not access or modify your actual analytics accounts
Requires user to provide context about current tools and goals
Does not replace specialized analytics engineering services

use_cases.title: Marketing Team Setup
use_cases.description: Design conversion tracking for paid campaigns with proper UTM discipline and attribution modeling.

use_cases.title: Product Analytics Foundation
use_cases.description: Build event taxonomy for SaaS products focusing on user journey and feature adoption metrics.

use_cases.title: Analytics Audit and Remediation
use_cases.description: Evaluate existing tracking for data quality issues and create prioritized fix recommendations.

prompt_templates.title: Beginner: Quick Tracking Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to track conversions for my small business website. We use Google Analytics 4 and Google Tag Manager. Help me set up conversion tracking for contact form submissions and newsletter signups. What events should I create and how do I verify they work?

prompt_templates.title: Intermediate: Event Taxonomy Design
prompt_templates.prompt: Our SaaS product needs a complete event tracking strategy. We have user signup, onboarding, feature usage, and subscription changes. Design an event taxonomy with naming conventions, required properties, and conversion definitions. Calculate our Measurement Readiness score based on our current state.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced: Analytics Audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit our existing GA4 setup. We track 50+ events but do not trust our conversion data. Identify data quality issues, attribution problems, and events that do not support decisions. Provide a prioritized remediation plan with expected impact on signal quality.

prompt_templates.title: Expert: Multi-Tool Strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: We use GA4 for marketing, Mixpanel for product, and internal data warehouse. Design a measurement strategy that ensures consistency across tools while avoiding duplicate tracking effort. Define which metrics belong in each tool and how to reconcile discrepancies.

output_examples.input: Small e-commerce site needs conversion tracking for purchases and add-to-cart events
output_examples.output: Tracking Plan with 4 core events: view_item, add_to_cart, begin_checkout, purchase_completed. Each event includes product_id, category, value properties. Conversions defined as purchase_completed (once per transaction) and add_to_cart (for funnel analysis). Measurement Readiness score: 72/100 after implementation.

output_examples.input: B2B SaaS wants to track demo requests and trial signups as conversions
output_examples.output: Conversion strategy defining demo_requested and trial_started as primary conversions. Funnel stages: pricing_viewed -> demo_requested -> demo_completed -> trial_started. UTM parameters preserved through multi-touch journey. Attribution model: position-based with 30-day window.

best_practices: Track only events that inform specific business decisions
Validate all events in real-time before publishing changes
Document every event with trigger conditions and property definitions
anti_patterns: Tracking everything just in case it becomes useful later
Treating GA4 default numbers as truth without validation
Optimizing dashboards while ignoring broken instrumentation

faq.question: What is the Measurement Readiness Index?
faq.answer: A diagnostic score from 0-100 that evaluates whether your analytics setup can produce reliable, decision-grade insights. Scores below 55 indicate broken data that should not be used for decisions.

faq.question: How many events should I track?
faq.answer: Track only events that map to specific decisions. Most businesses need 10-20 well-designed events rather than 100 noisy ones. Quality and accuracy matter more than volume.

faq.question: What makes a good conversion definition?
faq.answer: A conversion must represent real value, completed intent, and irreversible progress. Page views and button clicks are not conversions. Examples include signup_completed, purchase_completed, and demo_booked.

faq.question: How do I fix unreliable analytics data?
faq.answer: Start with the Measurement Readiness assessment to identify gaps. Fix data accuracy issues first, then clarify conversion definitions, then improve attribution. Validate each fix in real-time before moving to the next.

faq.question: Should I use GA4, Mixpanel, or both?
faq.answer: Use GA4 for marketing attribution and acquisition metrics. Use Mixpanel or Amplitude for product behavior and retention analysis. Define which questions each tool answers to avoid duplicate tracking effort.

faq.question: How often should I audit my tracking?
faq.answer: Audit quarterly or after major product changes. Review event firing rates for anomalies, validate conversion counts against business records, and remove unused events. Document every change with version control.
